Clara AI to answer calls, speak, understand, respond, and act to qualify customer intent. Clara will provide information, answer technical questions, and collect necessary details to book a meeting directly into the calendar.
changes.v2_value: During office hours, Clara will answer calls for new clients, small jobs, and service calls. She will qualify intent, collect necessary information, and provide service call fee details ($115 call out, $98/hour residential in half-hour increments) if the caller asks. If a caller insists on speaking to Ben, Clara will transfer the call to Ben's main business line (which will be his original number once he gets a new personal line). After hours, for non-emergency callers, Clara will take a message for the next business day.

v1_value: CRM: Jobber (Ben's Electric Solutions uses Jobber and finds it 'fantastic'). Clara needs to integrate with Jobber for scheduling appointments.

Previous phone system issues: Ben experienced problems with a prior phone system where an app assigned different numbers to team members, which appeared as spam, leading to calls not being answered. Clara's solution should avoid this issue.
v2_value: Initial call forwarding setup: Ben will configure call forwarding on his Android phone so that if he does not answer or declines a call, it forwards to Clara.

Future call forwarding setup: Once Ben's second personal number is active, Clara will be the first point of contact for the main business line. Clara will then transfer calls to Ben's original number (which will become his dedicated business line).
No CRM/Accounting tool (e.g., Jobber, QuickBooks, ServiceTitan) mentioned for integration.

changes.v1_value: Clara AI will answer calls 24/7. For general inquiries or non-emergency service requests, Clara will qualify the intent, collect customer name, address, phone number, and service details, and then book a meeting directly into the calendar. For specific emergency clients (regular property managers and general contractors, particularly for critical issues like gas station pump failures), Clara should route the call to Ben Penoyer, who is the designated on-call person. Other emergency calls from the general public will be deferred elsewhere.
changes.v2_value: During after-hours, if 'the one customer' calls, Clara will patch the call directly through to Ben's main business line. For all other callers, Clara will inform them that it is after business hours and will take a message to be addressed on the next business day.

changes.v1_value: Clara AI will answer calls during office hours. Clara will speak, understand, respond, and act to qualify customer intent for various services (e.g., outlet replacements, EV chargers, hot tub hookups, panel changes). Clara is capable of answering technical questions. Clara will collect customer name, address, phone number, and service details, and then book a meeting directly into the calendar. Clara is designed to handle multiple calls simultaneously and filter spam/sales calls. Notifications of booked appointments will be sent via email, text, and available on a dashboard.
changes.v2_value: During office hours, Ben currently takes all calls. Once Clara is live, initially, calls will forward to Clara if Ben doesn't answer or declines. In the long term, Clara will be the first point of contact for the main business line. Clara will answer calls for new client inquiries, small jobs, and service calls. She will qualify the caller's intent, collect necessary information, and provide service call fee details ($115 call out, $98/hour residential in half-hour increments) if the caller asks. If a caller insists on speaking to Ben, Clara will transfer the call to Ben's main business line (his original number).

v1_value: Ben's Electric Solutions is not generally equipped for emergency services for the public, but provides them for specific regular property managers and general contractors.
Ben Penoyer is the sole on-call person for emergencies.
Clara should be able to answer technical questions related to electrical work, as demonstrated in the demo.
v2_value: Clara should only mention service call fees ($115 call out, $98/hour residential) if the caller explicitly asks, not as a default statement.

changes.v1_value: Ben's Electric Solutions is owned by Ben Penoyer, an electrician with 30 years of experience, running his own business for 8 years. The company is growing, currently operating with 3 journeymen, 1 apprentice, Ben himself, and soon a new operations manager, plus subcontractors for overflow work. They operate 3 company vans and use 2 subcontractors, aiming for 5 vans by year-end. Ben currently handles all admin and back-end tasks, including office calls, but also performs customer-facing roles like quotes. He previously had negative experiences with virtual assistants and a phone system that assigned spam-like numbers. The business uses Jobber as its CRM, which Ben finds 'fantastic'. Clara AI was sold on a basic plan offering 500 minutes per month, but the exact price was not stated in the transcript. Ben committed to a three-month trial. A kickoff/onboarding call is scheduled for the next day at noon. Clara is expected to handle a wide range of electrical service inquiries, including technical questions, and is currently trained to book meetings directly. Notifications for booked appointments will be sent via email and text, and available on a dashboard. Clara is also expected to filter spam/sales calls. Emergency services are only provided for specific property managers and general contractors (e.g., gas station pump failures), with Ben Penoyer being the sole on-call person. General public emergencies are deferred.
changes.v2_value: Updated with onboarding data. Operational details confirmed.
